answer:
The augmented fourth above the subdominant in C major is F to B, and B is the dominant (G) of C major. The parallel key of C major is C minor, and the second note of the ascending chromatic scale in C minor is C♯, which is enharmonic to D♭, but the question asks for the second note of the ascending chromatic scale of its parallel key. In C minor, the ascending chromatic scale starts C, C♯, D, so the second note is C♯, which corresponds to ♯fa in solfège.

\boxed{D}
